mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining month over month, or if the sign effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, tighter financial conditions raise the equity risk premium and compress forward multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 1.91% on 2024-02-15, below the trigger threshold
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially lower than unconditional performance over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when unemployment rises sharply from its recent level.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return is not below zero across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals a broad demand slowdown that should translate into weaker corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is expanding while CPIAUCSL is also accelerating, firms face stronger nominal demand but higher input-cost pressure, and the margin squeeze should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while INDPRO rises
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of rising INDPRO plus accelerating CPIAUCSL do not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than neutral episodes, or if margins-and-valuation stress does not hold in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS signals improving household income and consumption capacity, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month PAYEMS gains are not associated with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in any multi-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration confirms recession risk and should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through weaker earnings and higher default risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined signal adds no predictive value beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST indicates improving housing demand and wealth effects that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, but the effect should fade once housing starts stop accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if positive HOUST momentum does not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns in recovery regimes, or if the effect persists equally when housing starts are flat or declining.
